title: Oligomeris subulata

It belongs to the genus Oligomeris, which is a group of plants characterized by their small, hairless leaves and purple flowers. The family Boraginaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are often found in dry, rocky areas. intro: Oligomeris subulata, commonly known as desert broom or desert broom weed, is a perennial shrub native to the deserts of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.
